Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 59 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Datahäxan 90 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. WebPKI and You 71 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. Printf-Tac-Toe 47 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 267 upvotes, 69 comments
  6. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 67 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 717 upvotes, 225 comments
  8. Galaxy Zoo 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 181 upvotes, 52 comments
  10. Are LLMs not getting better? 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3712 upvotes, 1386 comments
  12. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 596 upvotes, 212 comments
  13. 1B identity records exposed in ID verification data leak 79 upvotes, 23 comments
  14. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 102 upvotes, 33 comments
  15. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 26 upvotes, 5 comments
  16. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 326 upvotes, 137 comments
  17. BitNet: Inference framework for 1-bit LLMs 352 upvotes, 163 comments
  18. Thinnings: Sublist Witnesses and de Bruijn Index Shift Clumping 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged 249 upvotes, 129 comments
  20. Big Data on the Cheapest MacBook 26 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 44 upvotes, 17 comments
  22. Faster asin() was hiding in plain sight 220 upvotes, 120 comments
  23. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 303 upvotes, 179 comments
  24. Returning to Rails in 2026 184 upvotes, 104 comments
  25. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 69 upvotes, 36 comments
  26. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 294 upvotes, 203 comments
  27. Personal Computer by Perplexity 173 upvotes, 135 comments
  28. What Happens After You Die? (2016) 52 upvotes, 37 comments
  29. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 343 upvotes, 319 comments
  30. The MacBook Neo 564 upvotes, 902 comments

Last generated 8:30pm, 12 March 2026. Source here.
